steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the scale factor between similar rectangles
steps.content: Rectangle NPQR is similar to rectangle JKLM, with each side of NPQR being 7 times the corresponding side of JKLM. The linear scale factor is $k = 7$.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule for similar figures
steps.content: For similar figures with a linear scale factor of $k$, the area scale factor is $k^2$. Since $k = 7$, the area of NPQR is $7^2 = 49$ times the area of JKLM.
